title: Nagpur sizzles at 45°C as Vidarbha reels under intense heatwave - The Live Nagpur
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Maharashtra
full_text:
Vidarbha remained trapped in an intense spell of summer heat on Friday, with multiple cities recording temperatures above the 45-degree Celsius mark and hot winds sweeping across the region. Amravati turned into the hottest pocket of Vidarbha at 45.6°C, while Wardha closely followed at 45.5°C. Akola recorded 45.4°C and Nagpur touched 45°C, indicating that the severe heat conditions continue to tighten their grip over central India.
Several other districts also witnessed dangerously high temperatures through the day. Chandrapur registered 43.8°C, followed by Yavatmal at 43.6°C and Brahmapuri at 43.3°C. Gadchiroli recorded 42.6°C, Washim 42.5°C and Bhandara 42°C, while Buldana stood at 41.5°C. Gondia remained comparatively lower at 40°C, though the heat remained intense enough to disrupt normal daytime activity.
The soaring temperatures have visibly altered daily routines across Vidarbha, with roads wearing a deserted look during afternoon hours and residents preferring to stay indoors. The continuous heat buildup over the past few days has increased discomfort levels, especially in urban pockets where concrete structures and traffic congestion are adding to the oppressive conditions. Weather patterns indicate that dry and harsh winds are continuing to dominate the region, making outdoor exposure increasingly difficult.
Doctors and health experts have urged people to take extra precautions as prolonged exposure to extreme heat can trigger dehydration, fatigue and heatstroke. Citizens are advised to drink water frequently even if not thirsty, include electrolyte-rich fluids and seasonal fruits in their diet, and avoid stepping out between noon and 4 pm unless necessary. Experts also recommend wearing loose cotton clothes, keeping indoor spaces ventilated, and paying special attention to children, elderly people and outdoor workers who remain most vulnerable during prolonged heatwave conditions.

title: Hot weather conditions to continue in Mumbai, Thane and Palghar; IMD issues yellow alert - Mid-day
full_text:
Mumbai and parts of Maharashtra has been reeling in hot weather conditions. Representational Pic/File
Hot weather conditions will continue in Mumbai, Thane and Palghar district, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) stated in a weather bulletin, issuing a yellow alert.
The bulletin said that hot and humid conditions were very likely in isolated pockets of Mumbai, Thane and Palghar.
Earlier in the morning, the IMD predicted that Mumbai, Thane and Palghar was likely to witness partly cloudy skies during the morning hours, turning mainly clear by the evening.
The IMD has forecast a maximum temperature of around 36 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 27 degrees Celsius in Mumbai on Friday.
It stated that parts of Maharashtra including Raigad, Ratnagiri, Sindhudurg, Pune, Kolhapur, Satara, Sangli and Solapur were very likely to witness thunderstorm accompanied with lightning.
A yellow alert was issued by the IMD, stating, "Light to moderate rainfall and gusty winds (30-40 kmph) at isolated places was very likely."
The weather department issued a heatwave alert for Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar, Jalna, Parbhani, Hingoli, Akola and Amravati districts in Maharashtra. A yellow alert issued by the IMD said, "Heatwave in isolated pockets. Possibility of light to moderate rain accompanied with thunderstorm towards afternoon/evening."
As Maharashtra continues to grapple with an unrelenting heatwave, temperatures across the state soared well above normal on Thursday, prompting concerns among residents and authorities alike.
In Pune, the maximum temperature touched 38.6 degrees Celsius, marking a departure of 1.1 degrees above normal, while the minimum temperature settled at 25.7 degrees Celsius, which was 3.4 degrees higher than usual.
The situation was far more severe in other parts of the state. Vidarbha emerged as the hottest region, with Akola recording the highest maximum temperature in Maharashtra at a blistering 45.9 degrees Celsius, a significant 3.7 degrees above normal. Several other stations also reported extreme readings, including Wardha at 45.5 degrees Celsius, Jalgaon at 45.3 degrees Celsius, Nagpur at 44.0 degrees Celsius, and Parbhani at 43.9 degrees Celsius. Even Pune's Lohegaon airport area sizzled at 41.3 degrees Celsius. In contrast, the hill station of Mahabaleshwar provided some respite, recording the state's lowest minimum temperature of 20.2 degrees Celsius.

title: Nagpur Again Hits 45°C; IMD Issues Four-Day Yellow Alert for Several Districts - lokmattimes.com

full_text:
Nagpur Again Hits 45°C; IMD Issues Four-Day Yellow Alert for Several Districts
By Lokmat Times Desk | Updated: May 15, 2026 23:11 IST2026-05-15T23:08:44+5:302026-05-15T23:11:34+5:30
Nagpur: The city recorded a 45 degree Celsius for the first time this May, the city has been left ...
Nagpur: The city recorded a 45 degree Celsius for the first time this May, the city has been left utterly distressed by the scorching heat . This is the third time in this season that mercury has touched 45 degrees. The met has issued the warning yellow alert for the districts of Nagpur, Amravati and Wardha for the period between May 16 and May 19. The possibility of heatwave has been forecasted for this duration.
With Nagpur's temperature reaching 45 degrees Celsius for the first time this May, the city has been left utterly distressed by the scorching heat. This marks the third instance during the current summer season that the mercury has touched the 45-degree mark; consequently, the Meteorological Department has issued a 'Yellow Alert' for the districts of Nagpur, Amravati, and Wardha for the period between May 16 and May 19. A possibility of a heatwave has been forecast for this duration.
The administration has advised citizens to avoid direct exposure to the sun between 12:00 PM and 4:00 PM. According to the Meteorological Department, two active troughs have developed: one extending from East Madhya Pradesh through Vidarbha and Marathwada down to Karnataka, and another stretching from West Madhya Pradesh through Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and West Bengal into Bangladesh. As a result, clouds have been gathering in the sky towards the evening, and some areas have even experienced light showers. However, these conditions do not appear to have had any mitigating effect on the heat.
On Friday, as early as 11:30 AM, Nagpur's temperature had already reached 40.4 degrees Celsius. By 2:30 PM, it climbed to 43.4 degrees, and within just one hour thereafter, it crossed the 45-degree threshold. Notably, the intensity of the heat persisted despite the presence of clouds in the sky. Even after sunset, citizens continued to be plagued by the oppressive heat. There has also been a significant rise in night-time temperatures; over the past 24 hours, Nagpur's minimum temperature rose by 2.8 degrees, reaching 30.4 degrees Celsius. Additionally, minimum temperatures of 31.9 degrees Celsius were recorded in Wardha, 31.5 degrees in Akola, and 30.2 degrees in Yavatmal.
Meanwhile, on Friday, the highest temperature recorded across the country was in Amravati, at 45.6 degrees Celsius. This was closely followed by Wardha, which recorded 45.5 degrees, and Akola, which recorded 45.4 degrees. In four districts of Vidarbha, the mercury was recorded at 45 degrees Celsius or higher. Among the hottest days of the current season, temperatures of 45.4 degrees were recorded on April 26, and 45 degrees on April 27 and May 15. According to weather experts, the latter half of May is also expected to remain under the grip of intense heat, and there is little likelihood of significant relief from the scorching heat even in June.

title: Maharashtra Weather Alert For May 16: IMD Issues Yellow Alert for Heatwave and Rain in 12 Districts; Next 24 Hours Crucial - PUNE PULSE
full_text:
Rain Likely in Western Maharashtra and Marathwada While Vidarbha and Konkan Brace for Intense Heat
Maharashtra, May 15, 2026: Maharashtra is witnessing another major shift in weather conditions, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) issuing yellow alerts for both heatwave-like conditions and rainfall across multiple districts. The next 24 hours are expected to remain crucial as several parts of the state are likely to experience contrasting weather patterns.
The IMD has issued a yellow alert for intense heat in six districts — Thane, Raigad, Ratnagiri, Sindhudurg, Akola and Amravati. At the same time, rainfall alerts have been issued for Kolhapur, Satara, Sangli, Solapur, Latur and Dharashiv districts due to the possibility of thunderstorms and pre-monsoon showers.
Konkan Region Likely to Witness Hot and Humid Conditions
The Konkan belt, including Mumbai, Thane and Raigad, is expected to continue experiencing hot and humid weather conditions throughout the day. Maximum temperatures in these areas are likely to remain between 34 and 36 degrees Celsius, while minimum temperatures may range from 26 to 28 degrees Celsius.
The IMD stated that high humidity levels along coastal areas are expected to increase discomfort and make conditions more sultry. Ratnagiri and Sindhudurg districts are also likely to witness humid weather accompanied by isolated light rain and thunderstorm activity during afternoon or evening hours.
Vidarbha Faces Severe Heatwave Conditions
In Vidarbha, Akola and Amravati districts are expected to remain under severe heat stress, with daytime temperatures likely to rise between 41 and 43 degrees Celsius. Night temperatures may stay around 27 to 29 degrees Celsius.
Authorities have advised residents to avoid stepping outdoors during afternoon hours unless absolutely necessary due to the intense heat conditions.
Rain and Thunderstorms Expected in Western Maharashtra
The IMD has predicted a change in weather across western Maharashtra, particularly in Kolhapur, Satara, Sangli and Solapur districts. These regions may witness light to moderate rainfall accompanied by thunderstorms and gusty winds.
Maximum temperatures in these districts are expected to remain between 34 and 38 degrees Celsius, while minimum temperatures may range from 22 to 26 degrees Celsius.
Marathwada Also Likely to Receive Rainfall
Latur and Dharashiv districts in Marathwada are also likely to experience thunderstorm activity and light rainfall in isolated areas. Some locations may witness lightning and short-duration showers.
Meanwhile, districts including Pune, Ahilyanagar, Parbhani, Hingoli and Nanded have not been placed under any official alert. However, weather officials said partly cloudy skies and isolated light to moderate rainfall may occur in these regions as well.
Temperatures in these districts are expected to remain between 36 and 39 degrees Celsius during the day and 23 to 27 degrees Celsius at night.
IMD Advises Citizens to Monitor Weather Updates
The Meteorological Department has stated that fluctuating weather conditions are likely to continue across Maharashtra over the next two days. While some districts may experience rising temperatures and heatwave conditions, others are expected to receive pre-monsoon rainfall.
Citizens have been advised to stay updated with official weather forecasts and take necessary precautions against both extreme heat and thunderstorm activity.
